title: calliphyllon palustre
intro: Calliphyllon palustre, also known as bog calliphyllon, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae, which includes citrus and rue plants, and is native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and other ailments. The health benefits of Calliphyllon palustre include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. Calliphyllon palustre can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
